Brief initial connection call with David Klein. He acknowledged receiving previous outreach and expressed mild interest in pipeline visibility tools. Currently using Fireflies but finding limitations in deal analytics.

questions_asked.question: What's your biggest challenge with visibility into your sales pipeline right now?
questions_asked.answer: We're using Fireflies for call recording but it doesn't give us the deal-level analytics we need. Hard to see which deals are at risk.

questions_asked.question: How is your solution different from Fireflies?
questions_asked.answer: We go beyond transcription to provide AI-driven risk scoring, pipeline forecasting, and proactive deal insights that integrate with your CRM workflow.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to schedule qualification meeting. David Klein didn't answer, left voicemail confirming next week's meeting.

Qualification call with David Klein. Discussed DeltaScope's current state with 15-person sales team, manual forecasting process, and challenges scaling with growth. David is champion, mentioned need to involve SVP of Revenue and IT for procurement.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your current forecasting process—how do you predict which deals will close?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual right now. Sales managers update a spreadsheet weekly based on gut feel and rep updates. It's not scalable as we grow.

questions_asked.question: Who else would need to be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Caleb Carter, our SVP of Revenue, would be the final decision-maker. Kevin Schneider in IT handles vendor procurement and security review.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical timeline for tool decisions at DeltaScope?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 4-6 weeks if we like the solution. Caleb moves fast when he sees clear ROI.

objections: We're already paying for Fireflies, so there'd be some overlap in cost
next_steps: Send ROI calculator and competitive comparison
Schedule follow-up to discuss use cases in detail

touches.ai_summary: Second qualification call diving into specific use cases. David shared details about pipeline visibility needs across 19 sales reps, interest in automations and consulting support for implementation. Discussed budget range and timing for Q2 rollout.

questions_asked.question: For the 19 reps you mentioned, what would success look like in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Better forecast accuracy—right now we're off by 20-30% most quarters. And I want reps spending less time on admin, more time selling.

questions_asked.question: What's your budget range for a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: We're looking at $15-20K annually depending on the feature set and support level. Caleb wants consulting help with the rollout.

next_steps: Prepare technical evaluation demo focused on pipeline visibility
Share implementation timeline with consulting option

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email from David Klein confirming interest in moving forward. He requested demo for himself and Caleb, asked for materials comparing our solution to Fireflies.

questions_asked.question: Can you send the Fireflies comparison before the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, I'll send a detailed feature comparison highlighting our deal intelligence and forecasting capabilities that go beyond call transcription.

next_steps: Send competitive analysis deck
Schedule technical eval demo

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation demo with David Klein. Walked through platform features: AI call analysis, deal risk scoring, pipeline forecasting, CRM integrations. David engaged positively, asked detailed questions about automation workflows and reporting. Confirmed interest in automations_plus_consulting package.

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the automation workflows for our specific sales process?
questions_asked.answer: Highly customizable—our consulting team will map your current process and configure workflows tailored to DeltaScope's stages and criteria.

questions_asked.question: What resonated most with you in the demo?
questions_asked.answer: The deal risk indicators. Being able to see early warning signs before deals stall would be huge for us.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take with the consulting package?
questions_asked.answer: 4-6 weeks for full rollout with your team size. Includes training, workflow configuration, and CRM integration.

next_steps: Prepare proposal for 19 seats with automations and consulting
Schedule exec meeting with Caleb Carter

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with David Klein. Presented $16,400 ACV for 19 seats with automations_plus_consulting package. David comfortable with pricing, confirmed alignment with budget. Discussed implementation timeline and next step of exec meeting with Caleb.

questions_asked.question: Does this pricing align with what you discussed with Caleb?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, right in the sweet spot. He'll want to understand the ROI model and timeline in our meeting next week.

questions_asked.question: What's Caleb's main concern or focus area we should address?
questions_asked.answer: He'll want proof this drives revenue impact, not just better reports. Show him forecast accuracy improvement and time savings data.

next_steps: Prepare ROI-focused exec presentation for Caleb
Schedule exec meeting

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Caleb Carter (SVP of Revenue) and David Klein. Presented business case focusing on forecast accuracy improvement and revenue impact. Caleb engaged, asked tough questions about competitive differentiation from Fireflies and implementation risk. Overall positive reception but wants to review contract terms and involve procurement.

questions_asked.question: We're already using Fireflies—why should we switch versus just add analysts to interpret the data?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies captures conversation data, but doesn't provide predictive deal intelligence. Our AI proactively flags at-risk deals and forecasts pipeline, which would require a dedicated analytics team to replicate manually.

questions_asked.question: What's the risk if implementation doesn't go smoothly and we disrupt the team mid-quarter?
questions_asked.answer: Our consulting package includes phased rollout to minimize disruption. We typically pilot with 3-5 reps first, prove value, then scale. Implementation is low-risk with our white-glove approach.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer decision for you?
questions_asked.answer: Clear ROI within first quarter and seamless CRM integration. If you can prove 15% forecast improvement like the case studies, I'm sold.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline to make a decision?
questions_asked.answer: I want this in place by start of Q3, so we need to move in the next 2-3 weeks. Kevin in IT needs to do security review first.

objections: Need to justify switching from Fireflies given existing investment
next_steps: Send contract and security documentation to Kevin Schneider
Schedule procurement review meeting
Provide additional Fireflies competitive analysis

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with David Klein to discuss contract terms and address Caleb's questions about Fireflies overlap. David mentioned Caleb is supportive but wants to ensure contract flexibility. Discussed transition plan from Fireflies and potential discount to ease decision.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the annual commitment? Caleb wants option to scale seats mid-year.
questions_asked.answer: We can include quarterly true-up language so you can add seats as you grow without renegotiating.

questions_asked.question: Where are you on the Fireflies decision—are you looking to replace it entirely?
questions_asked.answer: Likely keep Fireflies for basic recording and use your platform for the intelligence layer. Caleb sees them as complementary for now.

objections: Pricing is slightly above budget if we're keeping Fireflies
next_steps: Explore minor discount or extended payment terms
Get Kevin's security review completed
Schedule final exec alignment call with Caleb

touches.ai_summary: Final executive alignment meeting with Caleb Carter and David Klein. Discussed contract terms, confirmed quarterly seat true-up provision, and addressed security review feedback from Kevin. Caleb comfortable moving forward, wants to finalize legal review and close by end of week. Agreed on implementation kickoff in two weeks targeting Q3 launch.

questions_asked.question: Kevin flagged data residency requirements—can you confirm where conversation data is stored?
questions_asked.answer: All data stored in US-based AWS infrastructure with SOC 2 Type II compliance. We can provide detailed data handling documentation for legal review.

questions_asked.question: Are we aligned on moving forward this week?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, assuming legal review is clean. David will coordinate signatures on our end.

questions_asked.question: Any last concerns before we move to contracting?
questions_asked.answer: Just want to make sure your team is ready for kickoff in two weeks. We need to hit Q3 launch.

next_steps: Send final contract to legal
Coordinate signatures with David
Schedule implementation kickoff meeting
